title: Analyze Impact of New or Modified Requirements on Existing Artifacts
description: The system shall analyze the impact of newly created or modified requirements on existing requirements, business rules, plans, integrations, test cases, source documents, and backlog items.

title: No Full Dataset Injection Into LLM Prompts
description: The system shall not inject the complete requirements dataset into every LLM prompt. The AI Impact Analysis engine shall retrieve relevant requirements and context as a bounded set. Full dataset injection is prohibited due to: high token consumption, latency, context-window limits, poor signal-to-noise ratio, and difficult reproducibility.

architectureDecision.requiredPattern: Full Dataset → Indexed Repository → Retrieve Relevant Context → LLM Impact Analysis

title: Indexed Repository for Requirements and Related Artifacts
description: The system shall maintain an indexed repository of requirements and related artifacts to support impact analysis retrieval. The index shall support metadata filtering, semantic similarity (vector), and graph traversal.

title: Hybrid Retrieval: Metadata Filtering, Semantic Search, Graph Traversal, Deterministic Rules
description: The system shall retrieve relevant requirements using a combination of four methods: metadata filtering (exact domain/entity matching), semantic similarity (vector RAG), dependency graph traversal, and deterministic business-rule matching. No single retrieval method is sufficient in isolation.

retrievalMethods.purpose: Known business-rule relationships (e.g., waiting period → coverage effective date)

title: Traceability Graph Linking Requirements to All Downstream Artifacts
description: The system shall maintain a traceability graph linking requirements to: business rules, plan configurations, enrollment workflow steps, carrier integration points, payroll interface specifications, test cases, source documents, and backlog items.

title: Traverse Traceability Graph to Identify Directly and Indirectly Affected Artifacts
description: The system shall traverse the traceability graph to identify all directly affected and indirectly affected artifacts when a requirement changes. Graph traversal shall include both forward (downstream) and backward (upstream) directions.

title: Retrieve Relevant Source Document Excerpts for Impact Analysis
description: The system shall retrieve relevant source document excerpts to support impact analysis. Only excerpts relevant to the changed requirement and its identified dependencies shall be included in the LLM context.

title: Dynamic Context Package Construction for Each Impact Analysis Request
description: The system shall construct a dynamic context package for each AI impact analysis request. The context package shall include: the changed requirement, direct dependencies, related requirements (top-K), related business rules, related plans, integration points, test cases, and source evidence excerpts.

contextPackageStructure.change: Changed requirement with attribute and old/new values
contextPackageStructure.relatedPlans: Plans that reference the affected attribute
contextPackageStructure.relatedTestCases: Test cases covering the affected attribute

title: Classify Impacts as Direct, Indirect, Dependency, Potential, or No Impact
description: The system shall classify each identified impact as: DIRECT (requirement explicitly changes), INDIRECT (downstream effect from changed requirement), DEPENDENCY (explicit traceability link exists), POTENTIAL (semantic similarity but no confirmed dependency), or NO_IMPACT.

title: Assign Impact Severity and Confidence Score
description: The system shall assign an impact severity (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFORMATIONAL) and confidence score to each identified impact. Severity and confidence together determine prioritization for human review.

impacts.reason: Coverage effective date depends on waiting period
New-hire enrollment window may require adjustment
Carrier eligibility effective date may change
Payroll deduction start date may change
Expected eligibility date in test must be updated
Employee-facing waiting period language should be reviewed

title: Evidence and Traceability Reference for Each Identified Impact
description: The system shall provide evidence and a traceability reference supporting each identified impact. Evidence shall include the related requirement or artifact ID, relationship type, reason for impact, and source document excerpt where applicable.

title: Distinguish Semantic Similarity from Business or Technical Dependency
description: The system shall distinguish semantic similarity from actual business or technical dependency. A requirement that is semantically similar to the changed requirement but has no explicit dependency or matching attributes shall be classified as POTENTIAL impact, not DIRECT or INDIRECT.

title: No Automatic Modification of Requirements Based on AI Impact Analysis
description: The system shall not automatically modify existing requirements based solely on AI-generated impact analysis. The AI shall recommend impacts; human authorization is required before any requirement change takes effect.

title: Human Review of AI-Generated Impact Assessments
description: The system shall allow authorized users to accept, reject, or modify AI-generated impact assessments. Human decisions shall be captured with actor, role, timestamp, and optional comment.

title: Audit Trail for Impact Analysis Results and Human Decisions
description: The system shall maintain an immutable audit trail of all impact analysis results, AI classifications, human review decisions, and accepted or rejected impact recommendations.

mvpArchitectureDecision.persistence: lowdb/JSON with traceability graph built in-memory from persisted relationships
mvpArchitectureDecision.vectorIndex: Embedding-based semantic retrieval for requirement similarity
mvpArchitectureDecision.metadataFilter: Exact domain/entity matching as primary retrieval layer
mvpArchitectureDecision.graphTraversal: Explicit dependency traversal from persisted traceability links
mvpArchitectureDecision.llm: Gemini for semantic impact reasoning using bounded context package
mvpArchitectureDecision.retrievalStrategy: Top-K retrieval — not full dataset injection
mvpArchitectureDecision.contextManagement: Rolling requirement summaries for high-level context
mvpArchitectureDecision.keyArchitecturePrinciple: The AI Impact Analysis engine shall use a hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach. The complete requirements corpus shall be indexed but shall not be injected into individual LLM prompts.
